channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Data Center Push: OpenAI Expands Stargate Project Across 16 US States
item.description: OpenAI's massive data center push, with the company scouting sites in 16 states across the US to power its AI technology and support the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Brazil introduces law restricting smartphone use in schools amid concern over the impact on learning
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools takes effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon Reports Better-Than-Expected Holiday Sales, but Stocks Dip on Disappointing Guidance
item.description: Amazon's revenue and profits for the holiday shopping period exceeded expectations, but its stocks fell in after-hours trading due to disappointing guidance for the current quarter.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump plan for federal worker resignations
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk, one of President Donald Trump's most powerful advisers, has orchestrated an unprecedented financial incentive for people to leave their government jobs, promising several months of pay in return for their resignation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Telescopes spy a monster radio jet streaming from a bright and early object in the universe
item.description: The jet of radio waves is the biggest ever detected so early in the history of the universe, astronomers reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oracle’s HR software now has AI agents to help with taxes and career planning
item.description: Oracle’s new AI agents will answer employee questions about everything job-related, from hiring to retiring.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Should you buy Nikon’s new Coolpix P1100? Here are 5 things you need to know about the updated 125x superzoom
item.description: Nikon's Coolpix P1100 boasts the world's best 125x optical zoom, but it offers very little that's new over the Coolpix P1000.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Spy vs spy: Security agencies help secure the network edge
item.description: The national intelligence services of five countries have offered enterprises advice on beating spies at their own game in a series of documents intended to help them protect network edge devices and appliances such as firewalls, routers, VPN (virtual private networks) gateways, internet of things (
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smart windows take a page from nature's pinecone playbook
item.description: An innovative new window system keeps your home comfortable without using a single watt of electricity and could change the way we think about energy efficiency in buildings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las empresas europeas están menos dispuestas a invertir en TI que las estadounidenses
item.description: Las grandes empresas estadounidenses con ventas anuales de más de 1.000 millones de dólares invierten el 1,45 % de sus ventas en tecnología y TI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kyndryl expands Palo Alto deal to offer managed SASE service
item.description: Kyndryl has expanded its alliance with Palo Alto Networks to add secure access service edge (SASE) services to its managed services offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Researchers link DeepSeek’s blockbuster chatbot to Chinese telecom banned from doing business in US
item.description: Experts say DeepSeek has computer code that could send some user login information to a Chinese state-owned company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google removes weapons development, surveillance pledges from AI ethics policy
item.description: Google has updated its ethical policies on artificial intelligence, eliminating a pledge to not use AI technology for weapons development and surveillance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A wedding necklace and Polish dumplings will be part of a mission to the International Space Station
item.description: What do a wedding necklace, Polish savory dumplings and a photo of Hungary’s first astronaut have in common.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cybercriminals' sneaky game plan to take advantage of Super Bowl fans
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the Super Bowl's huge audience makes it a prime target for many types of cybersecurity scams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIOs grapple with subpar global genAI models
item.description: With the number of generative AI trials soaring in the enterprise, it is typical for the CIO to purchase numerous large language models from various model makers, tweaked for different geographies and languages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Linux Foundation warns of US OFAC sanctions
item.description: Developers must be cautious about who they interact with and where contributions come from, warns a Linux Foundation blog post intended to help open-source developers navigate US OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thinking of moving off VMware? Gartner tallies cost of large-scale VMware migration
item.description: Broadcom’s $61 billion acquisition of VMware caused quite a stir in the technology industry and continues to prompt VMware customers to consider alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wheeled wonder robot dog shows off crazy dance moves in all kinds of tough terrain
item.description: Lynx the robot dog from China is pushing boundaries of what autonomous mobile robots can achieve with a groundbreaking design and unique mobility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is NaaS? Providers, delivery models, and benefits explained
item.description: Network as a service (NaaS) is a cloud service model that’s designed to let enterprise IT professionals order network infrastructure components from a menu of options, have them configured to fit their business needs, and have the whole thing delivered, running and managed in a matter of hours inste
item.dc:creator: Network World
